user_query:
Yesterday my nightly import of interview transcripts failed. Please triage the incident and make the dataset reliable again. Use the provided validator to reproduce the failure and base your summary on its output.

Requirements:
1) Reproduce and capture the failure: run
   python3 tools/transcript_validator.py --input input/transcripts.jsonl --schema input/schema.json
   Save the complete stdout/stderr to workspace/report/validation_before.txt.

2) Read the local files input/transcripts.jsonl, input/schema.json, and input/cities.csv to identify exactly which records and fields caused the failure. Produce a structured incident summary at workspace/report/incident_summary.json with these fields:
   - trigger_command: the full command used in step (1)
   - errors: list of objects {line_number, record_id, message}
   - affected_files: list of file paths you examined
   - root_cause: a concise explanation of why validation failed
   - fixes_applied: list of objects {record_id, changes} describing the minimal corrections you will make

3) Create a cleaned dataset that preserves every valid record unchanged and minimally corrects invalid records so that they pass validation. Use input/cities.csv for city-to-country lookups when country is missing. Normalize dates to YYYY-MM-DD. Resolve duplicate IDs by assigning a new unique ID that matches the schema’s prefix and digit rules. Write the cleaned dataset to workspace/out/transcripts_clean.jsonl.

4) Prove the fix: re-run the validator on the cleaned file and save the complete output to workspace/report/validation_after.txt using
   python3 tools/transcript_validator.py --input workspace/out/transcripts_clean.jsonl --schema input/schema.json
   The validator must report success (exit code 0) for the cleaned dataset.

Do not modify tools/transcript_validator.py. Your outputs (the two validation logs, the incident_summary.json, and the cleaned JSONL) should be sufficient for me to verify your work. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
